text: In the Aramaic text, ‘and (the king) drank wine in the presence of all the thousand’.

If the event here referred to is the well-known capture of Babylon by Cyrus, Darius the Mede should perhaps be identified with Gobryas, a general of his who took command of the city after its capture. It is not impossible that a short-lived ‘Median’ empire may have intervened between that of Babylon and that of Persia, but the monuments reveal no trace of it; and the sacred writers appear to regard the Medes and Persians as already, for political purposes, a single nation.
